title: Lasiorrhiza gracilis
taxonomy: Lasiorrhiza gracilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It belongs to the genus Lasiorrhiza, which is a group of flowering plants endemic to Australia. The species Lasiorrhiza gracilis is a type of climber or twiner, and it is native to the tropical regions of Northern Australia. Lasiorrhiza gracilis is further classified into the order Fabales and the subclass Rosidae. This classification helps to identify the plant's characteristics and relationships with other species within the same family and order.
uses: Lasiorrhiza gracilis has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians, particularly in Western Australia, where it has been employed in medicine for centuries.
